title: Alkaline Bromodeoxyuridine (BrdU) Comet Assay for DNA Damage Detection
author: Ribeiro, Londero, et al.
materials: Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO; Sigma‐Aldrich, cat. no. D5879‐1L)
Hydroxyurea powder (HU; Sigma‐Aldrich, cat. no. H8627)
Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium salt dihydrate (EDTA; Merck KGaA, cat. no. E5134)

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocol)
steps.instruction: UV‐C light source (254 nm) calibrated with a dosimeter (VLX‐3W radiometer)UV‐protective eyewear and shielding

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocol)
steps.instruction: Perform all steps described in the Basic Protocol, with the following modifications:

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocol)
steps.instruction:
XP‐V (XP30RO) fibroblasts: SV40‐transformed fibroblasts derived from an XP‐V patient, deficient in DNA polymerase η (Polη; POLH
−/−)XP‐V Comp (XP30RO complemented fibroblasts): SV40‐transformed fibroblasts derived from the same XP‐V patient, reconstituted with wild‐type POLH to restore TLS Polη function
UV‐C light source (254 nm) calibrated with dosimeter (VLX‐3W radiometer)Fibroblast culture medium: DMEM High‐Glucose + 10% FBS + 1% antibiotic/antimycoticUV safety shielding

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocol)
steps.instruction: Perform all steps described in the Basic Protocol, with the following modifications specific to XP‐V and XPV‐Comp cells.

steps.title: SYBR Green staining solution
steps.instruction: ReagentFinal concentrationQuantity or volumeSYBR Green I nucleic acid gel stain (10,000× concentrate in DMSO; Life Technologies, cat. no. S11494)1×1 µlTris‐EDTA buffer–9999 µlTotal10,000 µlJohn Wiley & Sons, Ltd.

steps.title: SYBR Green staining solution
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh on the day of use. In a sterile amber microcentrifuge tube (or a clear tube wrapped in foil), add 9999 µl of freshly prepared or prechilled 1× PBS. Carefully add 1 µl of SYBR Green I (10,000× stock) to obtain a 1:10,000 dilution. Mix gently by pipetting up and down—do not vortex. Always protect the solution from light. If not used immediately, store up to 6 months in a light‐protected container at room temperature.

steps.title: Comet analysis
steps.instruction: The alkaline BrdU comet assay allows accurate measurement of replication‐related DNA damage by distinguishing BrdU‐labeled DNA from the total genomic content. Selecting the appropriate comet type and fluorescence channel for analysis is crucial for precise interpretation (see Fig. 3).

steps.title: Comparative analysis/representations
steps.instruction: Compile the quantified data and calculate the mean ± standard deviation (SD) for both technical and biological replicates. Use GraphPad Prism (or equivalent software) to create either bar graphs (showing mean ± SD) or scatter plots (displaying individual data points). Predefine the threshold for statistical significance (e.g., p < 0.05). Figure 3D demonstrates the quantitative difference between these two approaches in a pilot experiment with RPE‐1 h‐TERT TP53 KO cells. HU treatment significantly increased Olive Tail Moment when quantified in the BrdU channel alone, highlighting replication‐associated DNA damage. In contrast, the SYBR channel alone or in combination with BrdU and SYBR Green measurement captured total DNA damage, but diluted replication‐specific effects. Together, these complementary readouts provide both a targeted and comprehensive perspective of DNA damage.

steps.title: An Excel template can be created...
steps.instruction: An Excel template can be created to calculates descriptive statistics for each sample, including mean, median, and standard deviation (SD). Data can be shown separately for standard comets and all comets, including outliers, depending on the experimental criteria.

steps.title: For data visualization, use either bar graphs or scatter plots
steps.instruction: For data visualization, use either bar graphs or scatter plots. (a) Bar graphs: Plot the mean Olive tail moment (± SD) for each biological replicate. Show BrdU‐only and BrdU + SYBR Green data in separate graphs. (b) Scatter plots: Display individual BrdU Olive tail moment values for at least 200 nucleoids per treatment, ensuring data from at least two independent experiments for each condition.

steps.title: For statistical analysis, use an unpaired...
steps.instruction: For statistical analysis, use an unpaired Student's t‐test for comparisons between two groups (e.g., NT vs. HU‐treated). For three or more groups, use one‐way ANOVA followed by Bonferroni's post hoc test (or a suitable alternative such as Tukey's post hoc HSD test). Verify assumptions of normality, or apply non‐parametric tests (e.g., the Mann‐Whitney U test or the Kruskal‐Wallis test) if needed.

steps.title: When publishing comet assay data, it...
steps.instruction: When publishing comet assay data, it is advisable to follow the Minimum Information for Reporting Comet Assay (MIRCA) guidelines (Mooler et al., 2020), which promote transparency, reproducibility, and consistency across studies.

steps.title: When applicable, briefly explain the rationale...
steps.instruction: When applicable, briefly explain the rationale behind the chosen statistical approach and discuss alternative methods if they are more appropriate for the data distribution or experimental design.
